purpose: Help the PPT AI Agent choose a broad dark analytics canvas first, then compose the final page in slides/*.tsx from reusable components based on page intent and grounded content.

source_theme.derived_from: the original Dark Analytics Charts template
visual_language: dark slate cover and closing pages
blue primary accent with teal and indigo secondary accents
chart, comparison, timeline, image evidence, and summary component systems
source_theme.relationship_to_v1: This canvas group keeps the v1 theme and component library, but does not copy v1's concrete concrete regional-comparison business page blueprints.

rules: Do not treat canvas files as finished slide structures.
Copy or derive the selected canvas into ./slides before adapting it for a concrete deck.
Use each canvas only as a branded frame before composing real content.

slides.role: Registered slide entries and final page implementations.
rules: group.json.layouts and manifest.json source.path must only reference this directory.
Final deck pages should replace canvas slot guides with page-specific component compositions.

components.role: Reusable dark analytics component library copied from the original Dark Analytics Charts template.
rules: Choose component families from page semantics, not visual convenience.
Prefer composing existing components before adding new ones.

data.role: Content data for demo or concrete decks.
rules: JSON carries content only, not page-structure decisions.
If layout or component choice is wrong, edit slides/*.tsx.

theme.role: Shared color, typography, and visual token definitions migrated from the original Dark Analytics Charts template.

blueprints.name: Cover Canvas
blueprints.composition_guidance: Keep the dark analytics frame, series label, headline, scope, and footer metadata. Replace the visual slot with a relevant image, compact metric cluster, evidence cue, or scoped report motif.

blueprints.name: Content Canvas
blueprints.composition_guidance: Use as the default body frame when the page plan does not demand a dedicated comparison lane or primary chart. Fill the body with cards, headings, metrics, images, matrices, timelines, or short narrative blocks.

blueprints.name: Comparison Canvas
blueprints.composition_guidance: Treat each lane as a scaffold, not a fixed card. Replace lane guides with TrendStatCard, MetricHighlightCard, CircularComparisonMetricCard, AdvantageBarList, ComparisonMatrixBoard, image evidence, or custom lane-level compositions.

blueprints.name: Chart Evidence Canvas
suitable_for: table or source note alongside a visual
avoid_for: pages with no primary visual anchor
blueprints.composition_guidance: Use ChartPanelShell plus a chart module for quantitative evidence, AnalyticsImageShowcasePanel for a visual asset, ComparisonMatrixBoard for compact evidence tables, or HorizontalMilestoneTimeline for chronology. Use the right rail for concise interpretation, caveats, KPI notes, or source context.

blueprints.name: Closing Canvas
blueprints.composition_guidance: Keep density low. Adapt the two callouts into final CTA, next-step cards, or concise follow-up information while preserving editable text.

selection_guide: Choose cover-canvas only for the opening page or an intentional title reset.
Choose content-canvas for ordinary pages where the Agent needs freedom to compose cards, text, image, table, timeline, or mixed modules.
Choose comparison-canvas when the page intent is explicitly to compare two or three entities, options, markets, products, capabilities, or scenarios.
Choose chart-evidence-canvas when one chart, table, image, screenshot, map, timeline, or other evidence asset should dominate the page and interpretation should sit beside it.
For timeline pages, choose chart-evidence-canvas when chronology is the main evidence anchor; choose content-canvas only when the timeline is one module among several.
For evidence pages, choose chart-evidence-canvas when one source-backed visual or table anchors the claim; choose content-canvas for multi-evidence summaries.
Choose closing-canvas only for the final thank-you, contact, decision request, or next-step page.
agent_workflow_notes: This template group intentionally provides canvas-level starting points, not finished industry pages.
Start by choosing the broad canvas family from page intent; do not start by copying a the original Dark Analytics Charts template business blueprint.
Before editing slides/*.tsx, read components/README.md and identify the component families that fit the page plan.
Derive final pages in slides/*.tsx, then replace slot guides with concrete component composition.
Use data/demo or deck JSON for content values only; layout, slot ownership, and component choice belong in TSX.
Do not paste source HTML or a concrete business page into a canvas. Rebuild the page from chart analytics components so it remains editable and PPT-friendly.
